framework.name: nizam-framework
framework.description: A generalised, AI-legible, versioned governance framework distilling documentation standards, planning and execution protocols, adversarial TDD, contract-first harness loops, durable agent state, and governance inheritance into a single portable payload any AI agent or engineering team can consume in any runtime, for any project.

modules.purpose: Documentation standard, governance inheritance protocol, agent governance framework, and universal anti-hallucination constraints every consumer repository inherits verbatim.
Planning enforcement, the contract-first execution harness loop, adversarial TDD, the universal circuit breaker, tool-driven durable state, and the release train protocol.
The JSON Schema this root index validates against, plus generalised scope-definition and registry patterns for consumer repositories building their own ecosystem-level registries on top of Nizam.
Consumer-repo document and manifest templates a bootstrapped repository copies and fills in with its own content.
JSON Schemas validating every machine-readable artifact the framework and its consumers produce.
The one unified, runtime-agnostic skill payload (manifest, instructions, adapter interface) any agent runtime loads to act on the framework.

Shipped framework documentation: Architecture Decision Records and the self-contained HTML user guide, distributed with the repository and rendered on GitHub or GitHub Pages (not part of the consumer-injected bootstrap payload).

capabilities.summary: The required frontmatter, status lifecycle, versioning, and machine-readability rules every governed document must satisfy.
How a consumer repository inherits the framework via pinned-tag cloning and bootstrap.sh, verifies inheritance, and detects drift.
The generalised multi-agent execution model: agent roles, the dual validator gate, and the JSON verdict parse rule.
Binds agent roles to task types, latency budgets, cost budgets, and safety classes rather than to specific models.
The mandatory 10-gate MERGE_READY formula that ensures no unverified code bypasses the contract-first execution loop.
Rules for integrating Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers: treating MCP as a capability plane, not a trust plane.
The seven canonical failure modes in agentic software engineering, with explicit detection and response protocols.
Rules for ensuring every artifact, prompt, model, tool call, and release is attestable and auditable.
Deny-by-default role permission classes and Kubernetes RBAC allocations for agentic systems.
The executable truth layer, the cross-repo query protocol, and the seven-tier architecture model for ecosystem-scale governance.
The universal AH-1 through AH-4 constraints binding every agent action, independent of protocol or task.
The mandatory pre-code product_spec.md + feature_list.json artifact pair and the Dependency Enforcement Rule.
The two-loop state machine (pre-code contract alignment, post-code implementation repair) and the JSON Verdict Parse Rule that gates every stage.
Evaluator independence, false-pass and false-fail hunting patterns, and the negative-testing requirement.
The single authoritative 3-strike attempt limit (DD-2) embedded by every repeatable execution loop.
Query-not-bulk-read discipline (DD-1) via the root index, and evidence externalisation to .agent/evidence/ (DD-3).
The verification hierarchy, trace capture requirements, and role-specific eval suites that guarantee quality as models and prompts change.
Semantic-version git-tag release cuts, changelog discipline, and the consumer upgrade path via re-bootstrap.
Treats model and prompt changes as code changes, routing them through the contract-first execution loop and blocking promotion until role-specific evals pass.
How the Planner updates the ecosystem dependency graph and blocks execution until upstream contract deltas are reviewed.
The machine-readable capability manifest for the single unified, runtime-agnostic skill payload (DD-4).
The single skill-instructions payload any agent runtime loads to plan, execute, gate, and durably record contract-first work.
The runtime-adapter contract mapping the framework's abstract operations onto any runtime's native tool primitives.

The repo-local NDS compliance validator (checks C1-C11): frontmatter schema + format, untagged-fence sweep, NIZAM.json index integrity, branding/endpoint leakage, bootstrap.sh sanity, module-README presence, version-bump-vs-changelog (NDS Sec 7), repo-wide path resolution, single-source-of-truth consistency, and dogfood schema validation of the .agent/ audit artifacts against the shipped schemas.

consumption_guidance: Query this index first. Locate the module, capability, schema, or template path that matches the task at hand, then read only that one file. Do not bulk-read an entire governance directory (standard/, methodology/, templates/, schema/, or tools/) for a single task; that is context exhaustion, the failure DD-1 remediates (see methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md). Use the 'capabilities' array to resolve a named concern to its single authoritative_source, the 'modules' array to find a module's key_documents, and the 'schemas'/'templates' arrays to resolve a machine-artifact path directly.

index_schema.description: The JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) this document, NIZAM.json, must validate against.

self_reference.description: This file. NIZAM.json indexes itself so the root context router always appears in its own capability map.
